title: Epic: Update CLI to latest hypercerts-lexicon v0.10.0
description:
## Why
The hypercerts-lexicon repo (https://github.com/hypercerts-org/hypercerts-lexicon) has evolved significantly since the CLI was last synced. Multiple NSID namespaces have changed, new record types have been added, existing record schemas have new/renamed fields, and the workscope tag schema has been restructured.

## What success looks like
- All NSID constants match the latest lexicon definitions
- All record creation/edit/delete/list/get commands produce records conforming to the latest schemas
- New record types (acknowledgement, contribution, badge, profile, organization) have CLI commands
- All existing tests pass, new tests cover changed field names and new record types
- `make build && make test && make lint` all pass

## Key changes identified
1. **NSID namespace moves**: measurement, attachment, evaluation moved from `org.hypercerts.claim.*` to `org.hypercerts.context.*`; collection moved from `org.hypercerts.claim.collection` to `org.hypercerts.collection`
2. **WorkScope tag**: field `label` renamed to `name`, field `kind` renamed to `category`, new fields: `status`, `supersededBy`, `sameAs`, `referenceDocument`; key pattern changed from hyphen-separated to underscore-separated; NSID changed from `org.hypercerts.helper.workScopeTag` to `org.hypercerts.workscope.tag`
3. **WorkScope CEL**: `` changed from `#workScopeCel` to `org.hypercerts.workscope.cel`; field `labels` renamed to `usedTags` (now array of strongRefs instead of string array); `createdAt` now required
4. **Measurement**: singular `subject` changed to plural `subjects` (array); new fields: `measurers`, `evidenceURI`, `commentFacets`
5. **Evaluation**: `evaluators` changed from plain DID strings to `app.certified.defs#did` objects (`{did: '...'}`)
6. **Funding receipt**: `from` changed from plain DID string to `app.certified.defs#did` object
7. **Contributor**: field `image` added (union of uri/smallImage)
8. **Collection**: `avatar` and `banner` fields added
9. **New record types**: `org.hypercerts.context.acknowledgement`, `org.hypercerts.claim.contribution`, `app.certified.badge.definition`, `app.certified.badge.award`, `app.certified.badge.response`, `app.certified.actor.profile`, `app.certified.actor.organization`

## Scope
- Update all NSID constants
- Update all record builders to match new schemas
- Update all display/list formatters for renamed fields
- Add new CLI commands for new record types
- Update tests
- Update AGENTS.md to reflect new structure

title: Update NSID constants in collections.go
description:
## Files
- internal/atproto/collections.go (modify)

## What to do
Update all NSID constants to match the latest lexicon definitions:

| Old Constant | Old Value | New Value |
|---|---|---|
| CollectionMeasurement | org.hypercerts.claim.measurement | org.hypercerts.context.measurement |
| CollectionAttachment | org.hypercerts.claim.attachment | org.hypercerts.context.attachment |
| CollectionEvaluation | org.hypercerts.claim.evaluation | org.hypercerts.context.evaluation |
| CollectionCollection | org.hypercerts.claim.collection | org.hypercerts.collection |
| CollectionWorkScopeTag | org.hypercerts.helper.workScopeTag | org.hypercerts.workscope.tag |

Add new constants:
| New Constant | Value |
|---|---|
| CollectionContribution | org.hypercerts.claim.contribution |
| CollectionAcknowledgement | org.hypercerts.context.acknowledgement |
| CollectionWorkScopeCel | org.hypercerts.workscope.cel |
| CollectionBadgeDefinition | app.certified.badge.definition |
| CollectionBadgeAward | app.certified.badge.award |
| CollectionBadgeResponse | app.certified.badge.response |
| CollectionActorProfile | app.certified.actor.profile |
| CollectionActorOrganization | app.certified.actor.organization |

Keep unchanged:
- CollectionActivity = org.hypercerts.claim.activity
- CollectionContributorInfo = org.hypercerts.claim.contributorInformation
- CollectionRights = org.hypercerts.claim.rights
- CollectionFundingReceipt = org.hypercerts.funding.receipt
- CollectionLocation = app.certified.location

## Dont
- Do NOT rename the Go constant names (e.g. CollectionMeasurement stays CollectionMeasurement)
- Do NOT change any other files in this task
acceptance_criteria:
AC1: All 5 renamed constants have their new NSID values.
AC2: All 8 new constants are added.
AC3: All 5 unchanged constants remain the same.
AC4: make build succeeds (compilation may fail in other files referencing old $type strings — that is expected and will be fixed in subsequent tasks).
AC5: File has no syntax errors.
close_reason: 602d2b5 Updated all NSID constants: 5 renamed, 8 new, 5 unchanged

title: Add badge commands (definition, award, response)
description:
## Files
- cmd/badge.go (create)
- cmd/root.go (modify) — add badge command group to command tree

## What to do
Add CLI commands for the three badge record types. These are grouped under a single `badge` parent command.

### Command structure
```
hyper badge definition create [--title <text>] [--type <text>] [--description <text>]
hyper badge definition ls [--json]
hyper badge definition get <id>
hyper badge definition delete [--id <rkey>] [--force]

hyper badge award create [--badge <at-uri>] [--subject <at-uri-or-did>] [--note <text>] [--url <url>]
hyper badge award ls [--json]
hyper badge award get <id>
hyper badge award delete [--id <rkey>] [--force]

hyper badge response create [--badge-award <at-uri>] [--response accepted|rejected] [--weight <num>]
hyper badge response ls [--json]
hyper badge response get <id>
hyper badge response delete [--id <rkey>] [--force]
```

### Record schemas

**Badge Definition** (app.certified.badge.definition):
```go
record := map[string]any{
    "$type":     atproto.CollectionBadgeDefinition,
    "title":     title,      // required, maxLength 256
    "badgeType": badgeType,  // required, maxLength 100
    "icon":      blobRef,    // required — for CLI, skip blob upload, accept URI placeholder or skip
    "createdAt": time.Now().UTC().Format(time.RFC3339),
}
// Optional: "description" (maxLength 5000), "allowedIssuers" (array of DID objects)
```

NOTE: The `icon` field requires a blob upload which the CLI does not currently support. For now, make the icon field optional in the CLI (even though the lexicon requires it) and print a warning that the record may not validate without an icon. This is a known limitation.

**Badge Award** (app.certified.badge.award):
```go
record := map[string]any{
    "$type":     atproto.CollectionBadgeAward,
    "badge":     buildStrongRef(badgeURI, badgeCID),  // required
    "subject":   /* union: either {"$type": "app.certified.defs#did", "did": "..."} or strongRef */,
    "createdAt": time.Now().UTC().Format(time.RFC3339),
}
// Optional: "note" (maxLength 500), "url" (uri, maxLength 2048)
```

**Badge Response** (app.certified.badge.response):
```go
record := map[string]any{
    "$type":     atproto.CollectionBadgeResponse,
    "badgeAward": buildStrongRef(awardURI, awardCID),  // required
    "response":   "accepted",  // required, "accepted" or "rejected"
    "createdAt":  time.Now().UTC().Format(time.RFC3339),
}
// Optional: "weight" (string, maxLength 50)
```

### Follow existing patterns
- Use `requireAuth`, `runSimpleGet`, `menu.Confirm`, `menu.SingleSelect`
- Follow the same code structure as other CRUD commands
- Write output to `cmd.Root().Writer`

## Dont
- Do NOT implement blob upload for badge icon (out of scope)
- Do NOT add edit commands (badges are typically immutable)
acceptance_criteria:
AC1: All three badge subcommands (definition, award, response) are registered under "hyper badge" in root.go.
AC2: Badge definition create produces a valid record with title, badgeType, createdAt.
AC3: Badge award create produces a valid record with badge (strongRef), subject (DID object or strongRef), createdAt.
AC4: Badge response create produces a valid record with badgeAward (strongRef), response string, createdAt.
AC5: All three have working ls, get, delete commands.
AC6: make build && make test && make lint pass.
close_reason: 1bc9889 Add badge commands (definition, award, response)

title: Add profile and organization commands
description:
## Files
- cmd/profile.go (create)
- cmd/root.go (modify) — add profile and organization commands

## What to do
Add CLI commands for the two actor record types. These use key "literal:self" meaning there is exactly one record per user (like a profile).

### Command structure
```
hyper profile set [--display-name <text>] [--description <text>] [--pronouns <text>] [--website <url>]
hyper profile get
hyper profile delete [--force]

hyper organization set [--type <text>] [--founded-date <date>] [--url <url> --url-label <text>]
hyper organization get
hyper organization delete [--force]
```
Alias for organization: `org`

### Record schemas

**Profile** (app.certified.actor.profile, key=literal:self):
```go
record := map[string]any{
    "$type":     atproto.CollectionActorProfile,
    "createdAt": time.Now().UTC().Format(time.RFC3339),
}
// Optional fields:
record["displayName"] = name       // maxLength 640, maxGraphemes 64
record["description"] = desc       // maxLength 2560, maxGraphemes 256
record["pronouns"] = pronouns      // maxLength 200, maxGraphemes 20
record["website"] = website        // uri format
// avatar and banner are union types (uri or blob) — support URI only for CLI
```

**Organization** (app.certified.actor.organization, key=literal:self):
```go
record := map[string]any{
    "$type":     atproto.CollectionActorOrganization,
    "createdAt": time.Now().UTC().Format(time.RFC3339),
}
// Optional fields:
record["organizationType"] = []any{"nonprofit", "ngo"}  // array of strings
record["foundedDate"] = normalizeDate("2005-01-01")       // datetime
record["urls"] = []any{
    map[string]any{"url": "https://example.com", "label": "Website"},
}
record["location"] = buildStrongRef(locationURI, locationCID)  // strongRef to location
```

### Key difference from other commands
These records use `key: "literal:self"` — there is only ONE per user. So:
- `create` is actually `set` (upsert): check if record exists, if so PutRecord, otherwise CreateRecord with rkey "self"
- `ls` is not needed (there is only one)
- `get` does not need an ID argument (always fetches "self")
- `delete` does not need an ID argument

### CreateRecord with specific rkey
Use the existing `atproto.CreateRecord` but pass rkey="self". Check how the current API handles this — if CreateRecord does not support specifying rkey, use PutRecord instead.

## Dont
- Do NOT implement blob upload for avatar/banner (out of scope)
- Do NOT add complex interactive forms — keep it simple with flags
acceptance_criteria:
AC1: hyper profile set creates/updates a record at rkey "self" with $type app.certified.actor.profile.
AC2: hyper profile get fetches and displays the self record.
AC3: hyper organization set creates/updates a record at rkey "self" with $type app.certified.actor.organization.
AC4: hyper org get fetches and displays the self record.
AC5: Both delete commands work.
AC6: Commands are registered in root.go.
AC7: make build && make test && make lint pass.
close_reason: 4d726f4 Add profile and organization commands with singleton record pattern (rkey=self)

title: Add shortDescription and shortDescriptionFacets to attachment display
description:
## Files
- cmd/attachment.go (modify)

## What to do
The attachment lexicon (now org.hypercerts.context.attachment) has added new fields that the CLI should support:

### New fields to support in create/edit
1. `shortDescriptionFacets` — array of richtext facets. Skip for now (no CLI facets support).
2. `description` — ref to Leaflet linear document. Skip for now (complex rich text).

### Verify existing fields match lexicon
The current CLI already handles:
- `title` (required) ✓
- `subjects` (array of strongRefs) ✓
- `content` (array of URI objects) ✓
- `contentType` (string) ✓
- `shortDescription` (string) ✓ — but verify field name matches (was it "description" before?)
- `location` (strongRef) ✓
- `createdAt` ✓

### Check field name: description vs shortDescription
The current CLI may use `description` where the lexicon now has `shortDescription` (maxLength 3000, maxGraphemes 300) as the short text field, and `description` as a Leaflet document ref. Check and fix if needed:
- If the CLI currently writes to `description` as a plain string, rename it to `shortDescription`
- The `description` field is now a ref to a Leaflet document — do not write plain strings to it

## Dont
- Do NOT implement facets or Leaflet document support
- Do NOT change the NSID (already handled)
acceptance_criteria:
AC1: Attachment create writes short text to "shortDescription" field (not "description").
AC2: Attachment list/get reads from "shortDescription".
AC3: No plain string is written to the "description" field (it is reserved for Leaflet docs).
AC4: make build && make test pass.
close_reason: 6749dd7 Use shortDescription field for attachment text

title: Update collection: add avatar, banner, shortDescription fields
description:
## Files
- cmd/collection.go (modify)
- cmd/root.go (modify) — add new flags if needed

## What to do
The collection lexicon (now org.hypercerts.collection) has added new optional fields:

### New fields
1. `avatar` — union of org.hypercerts.defs#uri or org.hypercerts.defs#smallImage. For CLI, support URI only:
   ```go
   record["avatar"] = map[string]any{"$type": "org.hypercerts.defs#uri", "uri": avatarURL}
   ```
2. `banner` — union of org.hypercerts.defs#uri or org.hypercerts.defs#largeImage. For CLI, support URI only:
   ```go
   record["banner"] = map[string]any{"$type": "org.hypercerts.defs#uri", "uri": bannerURL}
   ```
3. `shortDescription` — string (maxLength 3000, maxGraphemes 300). Check if the CLI currently writes to `description` as a plain string — if so, rename to `shortDescription`. The `description` field is now a Leaflet document ref.

### Verify field name: description vs shortDescription
Same issue as attachment — the CLI may currently write plain text to `description`. The lexicon now has:
- `shortDescription` (string, max 300 graphemes) — for plain text summaries
- `description` (ref to Leaflet linear document) — for rich text

If the CLI currently writes a plain string to `description`, rename it to `shortDescription`.

### Add CLI flags
- `--avatar <url>` — avatar image URL
- `--banner <url>` — banner image URL
- Add these to both create and edit commands

### Interactive form
Add optional avatar/banner URL inputs to the interactive form.

## Dont
- Do NOT implement blob upload for avatar/banner
- Do NOT implement Leaflet document support for description
acceptance_criteria:
AC1: Collection create/edit supports --avatar and --banner flags that store URI objects.
AC2: Plain text description is written to "shortDescription" (not "description").
AC3: Collection list/get reads from "shortDescription".
AC4: make build && make test && make lint pass.
close_reason: eccf257 Update collection: add avatar, banner, shortDescription fields

title: Add image field to contributor records
description:
## Files
- cmd/contributor.go (modify)
- cmd/root.go (modify) — add --image flag

## What to do
The contributorInformation lexicon now includes an optional `image` field (union of org.hypercerts.defs#uri or org.hypercerts.defs#smallImage).

### Changes
1. Add `--image <url>` flag to contributor create and edit commands
2. In the record builder, when image URL is provided:
   ```go
   record["image"] = map[string]any{"$type": "org.hypercerts.defs#uri", "uri": imageURL}
   ```
3. In the interactive form, add an optional image URL input
4. In the list display, show whether an image is set (e.g. "✓" or "-")

## Dont
- Do NOT implement blob upload for images
- Do NOT change any other contributor fields
acceptance_criteria:
AC1: Contributor create/edit supports --image flag that stores a URI object.
AC2: Interactive form includes optional image URL input.
AC3: make build && make test && make lint pass.
close_reason: 9dad32b Add image field to contributor records

title: Update AGENTS.md to reflect new lexicon structure
description:
## Files
- AGENTS.md (modify)

## What to do
Update the AGENTS.md documentation to reflect all changes from the lexicon update:

### Update ATProto lexicon collections table
Replace the current table with the updated NSIDs:

| Collection | CLI Command | Description |
|---|---|---|
| org.hypercerts.claim.activity | activity | Hypercert activity (core impact claim) |
| org.hypercerts.context.measurement | measurement | Impact measurement |
| app.certified.location | location | Geographic location (LP v1.0) |
| org.hypercerts.context.attachment | attachment | Evidence attachment |
| org.hypercerts.claim.rights | rights | Rights and licenses |
| org.hypercerts.claim.contributorInformation | contributor | Contributor identity |
| org.hypercerts.claim.contribution | contribution | Contribution details |
| org.hypercerts.collection | collection | Collection/project grouping |
| org.hypercerts.context.evaluation | evaluation | Third-party evaluation |
| org.hypercerts.funding.receipt | funding | Funding receipt |
| org.hypercerts.workscope.tag | workscope | Work scope tag |
| org.hypercerts.workscope.cel | - | CEL expression (embedded in activity) |
| org.hypercerts.context.acknowledgement | acknowledgement | Bidirectional link acknowledgement |
| app.certified.badge.definition | badge definition | Badge definition |
| app.certified.badge.award | badge award | Badge award |
| app.certified.badge.response | badge response | Badge response |
| app.certified.actor.profile | profile | Actor profile |
| app.certified.actor.organization | organization | Organization metadata |

### Update command tree
Add new commands: acknowledgement, contribution, badge, profile, organization

### Update field descriptions
- workscope: label→name, kind→category, key pattern uses underscores
- measurement: subject→subjects
- evaluators: now DID objects
- funding from: now DID object

### Update key patterns section
- workscope key: underscore-separated (was hyphen-separated)

## Dont
- Do NOT change any Go code
- Do NOT add information that is not yet implemented
acceptance_criteria:
AC1: All NSID values in the table match the latest lexicon.
AC2: Command tree includes all new commands.
AC3: Field descriptions are accurate for the updated schemas.
AC4: No references to old NSIDs remain.
close_reason: 5ca9ce9 Updated AGENTS.md with new lexicon structure: all NSIDs, command tree, and field descriptions

title: Update $type strings in all record builders to match new NSIDs
description:
## Files
- cmd/measurement.go (modify)
- cmd/attachment.go (modify)
- cmd/evaluation.go (modify)
- cmd/collection.go (modify)
- cmd/workscope.go (modify)

## What to do
After NSID constants are updated in collections.go, the hardcoded "$type" strings in record builders must also be updated to use the constant (or the new string value). Currently each record builder sets `"$type": atproto.CollectionXxx` which will automatically pick up the new value. However, some files may have hardcoded string literals for $type. Search all cmd/*.go files for any hardcoded NSID strings that no longer match and update them.

Specifically check and fix:
1. `measurement.go`: Any hardcoded `"org.hypercerts.claim.measurement"` → should use `atproto.CollectionMeasurement`
2. `attachment.go`: Any hardcoded `"org.hypercerts.claim.attachment"` → should use `atproto.CollectionAttachment`
3. `evaluation.go`: Any hardcoded `"org.hypercerts.claim.evaluation"` → should use `atproto.CollectionEvaluation`
4. `collection.go`: Any hardcoded `"org.hypercerts.claim.collection"` → should use `atproto.CollectionCollection`
5. `workscope.go`: Any hardcoded `"org.hypercerts.helper.workScopeTag"` → should use `atproto.CollectionWorkScopeTag`

Also update the `$type` in the workscope CEL builder (in activity.go or util.go):
- Old: `"$type": "org.hypercerts.claim.activity#workScopeCel"` 
- New: `"$type": "org.hypercerts.workscope.cel"` (it is now a standalone object type, not a def under activity)

And update the workscope string $type if needed:
- `"$type": "org.hypercerts.claim.activity#workScopeString"` stays the same (it is still a def under activity)

## Dont
- Do NOT change field names or record structure in this task (that is handled in separate tasks)
- Do NOT add new commands
acceptance_criteria:
AC1: No hardcoded old NSID strings remain in any cmd/*.go file (grep for the 5 old values returns zero matches).
AC2: The workscope CEL $type is "org.hypercerts.workscope.cel".
AC3: make build succeeds.
AC4: make test passes.
close_reason: 399c4e4 Updated workscope CEL $type from atproto.CollectionActivity + "#workScopeCel" to atproto.CollectionWorkScopeCel. All other record builders already use constants correctly.

title: Update workscope tag: rename label→name, kind→category, update key pattern
description:
## Files
- cmd/workscope.go (modify)
- cmd/workscope_test.go (modify)
- cmd/root.go (modify) — update flag names if needed

## What to do
The workscope tag lexicon (org.hypercerts.workscope.tag) has renamed fields and changed the key format:

### Field renames
1. `label` → `name` (the human-readable display name)
2. `kind` → `category` (the category type)

### Key pattern change
- Old: lowercase-hyphenated `^[a-z0-9]+(-[a-z0-9]+)*$` (e.g. "climate-action")
- New: lowercase-underscore `^[a-z0-9]+(_[a-z0-9]+)*$` (e.g. "mangrove_restoration")
- Update `keyPattern` regex in workscope.go
- Update the description text in the form from "lowercase-hyphenated" to "lowercase, underscore-separated"

### New optional fields to support
- `status` (string, knownValues: "proposed", "accepted", "deprecated")
- `supersededBy` (strongRef to another workscope tag, only when status=deprecated)
- `sameAs` (array of URI strings, max 20)
- `referenceDocument` (union of org.hypercerts.defs#uri or org.hypercerts.defs#smallBlob — for CLI, support URI only)

### CLI flag renames
- `--label` flag → `--name` flag (in root.go workscope create/edit commands)
- `--kind` flag → `--category` flag
- Add `--status` flag

### Update all references
- In `workScopeOption` struct: rename `Label` field to `Name`, `Kind` to `Category`
- In `fetchWorkScopes`: read `name` instead of `label`, `category` instead of `kind`
- In `runWorkScopeCreate`: write `name` instead of `label`, `category` instead of `kind`
- In `runWorkScopeEdit`: read/write `name` instead of `label`, `category` instead of `kind`
- In `runWorkScopeList`: display `name` instead of `label`, `category` instead of `kind`; rename column headers
- In `selectWorkScope`: use `Name` instead of `Label` for display
- In `runWorkScopeDelete`: use `Name` instead of `Label` for display

### Update tests
- In workscope_test.go: update TestKeyPattern test cases to use underscores instead of hyphens
  - Valid: "climate_action", "mangrove_restoration", "sdg_13"
  - Invalid: "climate-action" (now invalid — hyphens not allowed)

## Dont
- Do NOT change the Go constant name CollectionWorkScopeTag (already updated in task 1)
- Do NOT modify the workscope CEL builder (that is in activity.go, handled separately)
acceptance_criteria:
AC1: keyPattern regex is ^[a-z0-9]+(_[a-z0-9]+)*$ and TestKeyPattern passes with underscore-valid and hyphen-invalid cases.
AC2: Record builder writes "name" not "label", "category" not "kind".
AC3: List output shows NAME and CATEGORY column headers.
AC4: CLI flags are --name and --category (not --label and --kind).
AC5: workScopeOption struct uses Name and Category fields.
AC6: make build && make test && make lint all pass.
close_reason: d1b4cdb Update workscope tag: rename label→name, kind→category, update key pattern

title: Update workscope CEL builder: labels→usedTags with strongRefs
description:
## Files
- cmd/activity.go (modify) — the buildWorkScopeCel function and any callers
- cmd/activityform.go (modify) — if CEL building happens here
- cmd/root.go (modify) — if --work-scope-cel flag description needs updating

## What to do
The workscope CEL object (org.hypercerts.workscope.cel) has changed:

### Changes
1. **$type**: was `"org.hypercerts.claim.activity#workScopeCel"`, now `"org.hypercerts.workscope.cel"` (already handled in task 2, but verify)
2. **Field rename**: `labels` → `usedTags`
3. **Type change**: `usedTags` is now an array of strongRefs (`[{"uri": "at://...", "cid": "..."}]`) instead of an array of plain strings
4. **New required field**: `createdAt` (datetime string)

### Implementation
The `buildWorkScopeCel()` function currently builds:
```go
map[string]any{
    "$type":      "org.hypercerts.claim.activity#workScopeCel",
    "expression": "scope.hasAll([...])",
    "labels":     []any{"label1", "label2"},
    "version":    "v1",
}
```

It needs to become:
```go
map[string]any{
    "$type":      "org.hypercerts.workscope.cel",
    "expression": "scope.hasAll([...])",
    "usedTags":   []any{strongRef1, strongRef2, ...},
    "version":    "v1",
    "createdAt":  time.Now().UTC().Format(time.RFC3339),
}
```

Since usedTags now requires strongRefs to actual workscope.tag records, the --work-scope-cel flag flow needs to:
1. Accept tag keys as input (same as before)
2. Look up the corresponding workscope.tag records by key to get their URIs and CIDs
3. Build strongRefs for each tag
4. If a tag key does not exist as a record, either error or skip with a warning

For the interactive flow, the activity form already collects workScope as a string. If the user enters CEL mode, the form should let them select existing workscope tags (using selectWorkScope or a multi-select) and build the CEL expression + usedTags from those selections.

### Key pattern in CEL expressions
Since workscope tag keys now use underscores (not hyphens), the CEL expression builder should generate expressions like:
`scope.hasAll(["mangrove_restoration", "environmental_education"])`

## Dont
- Do NOT change the workscope tag CRUD commands (handled in task 3)
- Do NOT change the workScopeString builder (it stays the same)
acceptance_criteria:
AC1: buildWorkScopeCel produces records with $type "org.hypercerts.workscope.cel".
AC2: The field is named "usedTags" (not "labels").
AC3: usedTags contains strongRef objects ({uri, cid}), not plain strings.
AC4: createdAt is included in the CEL object.
AC5: make build succeeds.
AC6: make test passes.
close_reason: 23294de Updated buildWorkScopeCel to use usedTags field with strongRefs, added createdAt, updated validation and docs

title: Update measurement: subject→subjects (array), add new fields
description:
## Files
- cmd/measurement.go (modify)
- cmd/root.go (modify) — update flag descriptions if needed
- cmd/activity.go (modify) — update cascading delete to scan for "subjects" instead of "subject"

## What to do
The measurement lexicon has changed from org.hypercerts.claim.measurement to org.hypercerts.context.measurement with these field changes:

### Breaking change: subject → subjects
- Old: `subject` (single strongRef `{uri, cid}`)
- New: `subjects` (array of strongRefs `[{uri, cid}, ...]`)
- The CLI currently links a measurement to ONE activity. Keep this UX but store it as a single-element array:
  ```go
  record["subjects"] = []any{buildStrongRef(uri, cid)}
  ```
- Update the `--activity` flag handler to build an array
- Update the interactive flow to build an array

### New optional fields to add
1. `measurers` — array of DID objects `[{"$type": "app.certified.defs#did", "did": "did:plc:..."}]`. Add a `--measurer` flag (can be specified multiple times or comma-separated). In interactive mode, default to the current users DID.
2. `evidenceURI` — array of URI strings. Add a `--evidence-uri` flag.
3. `commentFacets` — array of richtext facets for the comment field. Skip for now (no CLI support for facets).

### Update cascading delete in activity.go
The `findLinkedURIs` function in activity.go scans measurement records for a matching `subject` field. Update it to scan `subjects` (array) instead:
- Old: checks if `record["subject"]["uri"] == activityURI`
- New: checks if any element in `record["subjects"]` array has `uri == activityURI`

### Update measurement list display
- If the list display shows the linked activity, update it to read from `subjects[0]` instead of `subject`

## Dont
- Do NOT add multi-activity linking UI (keep single-activity UX, just store as array)
- Do NOT implement facets support
acceptance_criteria:
AC1: Measurement create writes "subjects" as an array of strongRefs (not singular "subject").
AC2: Measurement edit preserves the "subjects" array format.
AC3: Cascading delete in activity.go correctly scans "subjects" array for matching URIs.
AC4: Measurement list/get correctly reads from "subjects" array.
AC5: make build && make test pass.
close_reason: 710eaf8 Update measurement: subject→subjects (array), add measurers/evidenceURI fields

title: Update evaluation: evaluators to DID objects, score as nested def

### score field

Verify it still works and add `"$type": "org.hypercerts.context.evaluation#score"` if needed (ATProto convention for inline defs).

### Verify other fields

- `content` stays as array of URI objects (unchanged)
- `measurements` stays as array of strongRefs (unchanged)
- `location` stays as single strongRef (unchanged)

## Dont

- Do NOT add new CLI commands

AC2: Evaluator display correctly reads the "did" field from each object.

title: Add acknowledgement command (new record type)
description:
## Files
- cmd/acknowledgement.go (create)
- cmd/root.go (modify) — add acknowledgement command to command tree

## What to do
Add a new CLI command for the org.hypercerts.context.acknowledgement record type. This record enables bidirectional linking — when someone includes your record in their collection or activity, you can acknowledge or reject it.

### Command structure
```
hyper acknowledgement create [--subject <at-uri>] [--context <at-uri>] [--acknowledged] [--comment <text>]
hyper acknowledgement delete [--id <rkey>] [--force]
hyper acknowledgement ls [--json]
hyper acknowledgement get <id>
```
Alias: `ack`

### Record schema
```go
record := map[string]any{
    "$type":        atproto.CollectionAcknowledgement,
    "subject":      buildStrongRef(subjectURI, subjectCID),  // required
    "acknowledged": true,                                      // required, boolean
    "createdAt":    time.Now().UTC().Format(time.RFC3339),     // required
}
// Optional:
record["context"] = buildStrongRef(contextURI, contextCID)  // or {"$type": "org.hypercerts.defs#uri", "uri": "..."}
record["comment"] = "some comment"  // maxLength 10000, maxGraphemes 1000
```

### Interactive flow
1. Prompt for subject AT-URI (required) — get the record to look up its CID
2. Ask acknowledged: true/false (default true)
3. Optionally add context AT-URI
4. Optionally add comment

### Non-interactive flags
- `--subject` (required, AT-URI)
- `--context` (optional, AT-URI)
- `--acknowledged` (boolean flag, default true)
- `--rejected` (boolean flag, sets acknowledged=false)
- `--comment` (optional string)

### List display
Show columns: ID, SUBJECT, ACKNOWLEDGED, CONTEXT, CREATED

### Follow existing patterns
- Use `requireAuth`, `runSimpleGet`, `menu.Confirm`, `menu.SingleSelect` etc.
- Follow the same code structure as attachment.go or evaluation.go
- Write output to `cmd.Root().Writer`

## Dont
- Do NOT add edit command (acknowledgements are typically immutable — delete and recreate)
- Do NOT implement rich-text facets for comment
acceptance_criteria:
AC1: hyper acknowledgement create produces a valid org.hypercerts.context.acknowledgement record with subject (strongRef), acknowledged (bool), and createdAt.
AC2: hyper ack ls lists acknowledgement records with ID, SUBJECT, ACKNOWLEDGED columns.
AC3: hyper ack get <id> displays the full record JSON.
AC4: hyper ack delete works with interactive select and --id flag.
AC5: Command is registered in root.go command tree with alias "ack".
AC6: make build && make test && make lint pass.
close_reason: 822ea20 Add acknowledgement command with create/delete/ls/get subcommands and ack alias

title: Add contribution command (new record type)
description:
## Files
- cmd/contribution.go (create)
- cmd/root.go (modify) — add contribution command to command tree

## What to do
Add a new CLI command for the org.hypercerts.claim.contribution record type. This is a standalone record for contribution details that can be referenced from activity contributor entries via strongRef.

### Command structure
```
hyper contribution create [--role <text>] [--description <text>] [--start-date <date>] [--end-date <date>]
hyper contribution edit <id> [--role <text>] [--description <text>]
hyper contribution delete [--id <rkey>] [--force]
hyper contribution ls [--json]
hyper contribution get <id>
```

### Record schema
```go
record := map[string]any{
    "$type":     atproto.CollectionContribution,
    "createdAt": time.Now().UTC().Format(time.RFC3339),  // required
}
// All other fields are optional:
record["role"] = "Project Lead"                          // maxLength 100
record["contributionDescription"] = "Led the team..."   // maxLength 10000, maxGraphemes 1000
record["startDate"] = normalizeDate("2024-01-01")        // datetime
record["endDate"] = normalizeDate("2024-12-31")          // datetime
```

### Interactive flow
1. Prompt for role (optional)
2. Prompt for contribution description (optional)
3. Prompt for start date (optional, YYYY-MM-DD)
4. Prompt for end date (optional, YYYY-MM-DD)

### List display
Show columns: ID, ROLE, DESCRIPTION (truncated), START, END, CREATED

### Follow existing patterns
- Use `requireAuth`, `runSimpleGet`, `normalizeDate`, `menu.Confirm`
- Follow the same code structure as rights.go (simple CRUD)
- Write output to `cmd.Root().Writer`

## Dont
- Do NOT link contributions to activities in this task (that linking is done via the activity contributor array)
- Do NOT add complex interactive flows
acceptance_criteria:
AC1: hyper contribution create produces a valid org.hypercerts.claim.contribution record with createdAt.
AC2: All optional fields (role, contributionDescription, startDate, endDate) are correctly stored when provided.
AC3: hyper contribution ls displays records in a table.
AC4: hyper contribution get/edit/delete work correctly.
AC5: Command is registered in root.go command tree.
AC6: make build && make test && make lint pass.
close_reason: db50b5a Add contribution command with full CRUD operations

title: Blocker: hypercerts-cli-kxi.5 — Environment issues: tests hang, builds timeout, edits don't persist
description: Tried: Implementing huh.ErrUserAborted handling across all 9 cmd/*.go files. Applied 40+ edits using mcp_edit tool. Error: Tests hang indefinitely (go test timeout after 180s), builds timeout (go build timeout after 120s), file edits don't persist to disk (git status shows clean working tree immediately after edits, file modification times show later timestamps than edit operations). Likely cause: Docker/container filesystem sync issue, or Go toolchain corruption in build cache. The code changes themselves are correct - all .Run() calls on huh forms need errors.Is(err, huh.ErrUserAborted) checks added with fmt.Errorf('cancelled') returns. Files affected: cmd/activity.go, cmd/measurement.go, cmd/funding.go, cmd/rights.go, cmd/attachment.go, cmd/collection.go, cmd/evaluation.go, cmd/contributor.go, cmd/workscope.go.
close_reason: 19db673 Environment issue resolved, retrying

title: Epic: Add workScopeCel support to hc activity commands
description: The hc CLI currently only supports workScopeString (free-form text) for the activity workScope field. The CEL playground (hypercerts-cel-playground) defines a structured workScopeCel format with expression, labels[], and version that enables CEL-based querying of workscopes. We need to add --work-scope-cel flag to activity create/edit so users can set CEL-queryable workscopes. This unblocks using CEL to filter/query hypercerts by work scope tags.
close_reason: 4bb5e65 — workScopeCel flag implemented, activity updated with CEL workscope

title: Add --work-scope-cel flag to activity create and edit
acceptance_criteria:
1. `hc activity create --work-scope-cel "open-source,cli-development" --title "test" --description "test"` creates an activity with workScope of type `#workScopeCel` containing expression, labels array, and version
2. `hc activity edit <id> --work-scope-cel "tag-one,tag-two,tag-three"` updates the workScope to a workScopeCel record
3. Single label: `--work-scope-cel "open-source"` generates expression `scope.has("open-source")`
4. Multiple labels: `--work-scope-cel "a,b,c"` generates expression `scope.hasAll(['a', 'b', 'c'])`
5. Invalid key (uppercase, spaces, underscores) returns an error
6. Empty input returns an error
7. Duplicate labels are deduplicated
8. `hc activity ls` shows comma-joined label keys in the SCOPE column for activities with workScopeCel
9. `make build` passes without errors
10. `make test` passes without errors
11. `make lint` passes without errors
close_reason: 4bb5e65 add workScopeCel flag

title: Fix: activity list still reads 'labels' from old CEL schema
description:
## Problem
The activity list command (cmd/activity.go line 818) still reads 'labels' from workScopeCel records. After hypercerts-cli-0p7.4, the field was renamed to 'usedTags' and changed from string array to strongRef array.

## Evidence
```go
// cmd/activity.go:818
if labels, ok := ws["labels"].([]any); ok {
    var keys []string
    for _, l := range labels {
        if s, ok := l.(string); ok {
            keys = append(keys, s)
        }
    }
    scope = strings.Join(keys, ",")
}
```

## Expected behavior
Should read 'usedTags' and extract tag keys from strongRefs. Should also handle backward compatibility with old 'labels' format.

## Fix
Update activity list display to:
1. Try reading 'usedTags' first (new schema)
2. For each strongRef in usedTags, extract the tag key from the URI
3. Fall back to 'labels' for old records (backward compat)

## Files
- cmd/activity.go (modify, line ~818)

## Discovered from
hypercerts-cli-0p7.4
close_reason: 1b95fa3 Updated activity list to read usedTags (strongRefs) from new CEL schema, with backward compat for old labels field

title: Epic: P1 bug fixes and security improvements
description: Fix critical bugs found during code review: broken goreleaser config, wrong golangci prefix, plaintext password storage, double API calls, missing LICENSE, unhandled ErrUserAborted, and partial result loss on cancel.
close_reason: ea2683c All 7 P1 tasks complete — bugs fixed, security documented

title: Fix .goreleaser.yaml: wrong module path, entry point, and binary name
description:
## Files
- .goreleaser.yaml (modify)

## What to do

The goreleaser config has 3 errors that prevent releases from building:

1. **`main: .`** → should be `./cmd/hc` (the actual entry point)
2. **`binary: hyper`** → should be `hc` (matches Makefile line 6, README, and actual usage)
3. **`ldflags` path** references `github.com/hypercerts-org/hyper/cmd.Version` → should be `github.com/GainForest/hypercerts-cli/cmd.Version`

### Current (broken):
```yaml
builds:
  - main: .
    binary: hyper
    ldflags:
      - -s -w -X github.com/hypercerts-org/hyper/cmd.Version={{.Version}}
```

### Fixed:
```yaml
builds:
  - main: ./cmd/hc
    binary: hc
    ldflags:
      - -s -w -X github.com/GainForest/hypercerts-cli/cmd.Version={{.Version}}
```

## Test

```bash
grep "main: ./cmd/hc" .goreleaser.yaml && grep "binary: hc" .goreleaser.yaml && grep "GainForest/hypercerts-cli" .goreleaser.yaml
```

All three greps must match (exit 0).

## Dont
- Do NOT change any other goreleaser settings (archives, changelog, etc.)
- Do NOT run goreleaser — just fix the config
close_reason: 2f3c842 fix: correct .goreleaser.yaml entry point, binary name, and module path

title: Fix fetchAttachmentsForActivity double API call
description:
## Files
- cmd/attachment.go (modify)

## What to do

`fetchAttachmentsForActivity` (around line 99-128) makes two identical API calls to list all attachment records. The function calls `fetchAttachments()` (which internally calls `atproto.ListAllRecords`) and then calls `atproto.ListAllRecords` again directly.

### Current (broken):
```go
func fetchAttachmentsForActivity(ctx context.Context, client *atclient.APIClient, did string, activityCID string) ([]attachmentOption, error) {
    all, err := fetchAttachments(ctx, client, did)  // 1st API call
    if err != nil {
        return nil, err
    }
    // ... filter all by activityCID ...

    entries, _ := atproto.ListAllRecords(ctx, client, did, atproto.CollectionAttachment)  // 2nd identical API call!
    // ... uses entries for something ...
```

### Fix:
Remove the second `ListAllRecords` call. Use the data already fetched by `fetchAttachments`. If the second call was fetching raw entries for a different purpose (e.g., to get CIDs or URIs not in the option struct), refactor `fetchAttachments` to return the needed data, or extract the raw entries from the first call.

Read the function carefully to understand what data the second call provides that the first doesnt, then eliminate the redundancy.

## Test

```bash
make test
```

Must exit 0. Also verify with grep that there is only one `ListAllRecords.*CollectionAttachment` call path in the function.

## Dont
- Do NOT change the function signature
- Do NOT change the filtering logic
- Do NOT change other functions in attachment.go
close_reason: e533858 Eliminated double API call in fetchAttachmentsForActivity

title: Handle huh.ErrUserAborted in all interactive form .Run() calls
description:
## Files
- cmd/activity.go (modify)
- cmd/measurement.go (modify)
- cmd/funding.go (modify)
- cmd/rights.go (modify)
- cmd/attachment.go (modify)
- cmd/collection.go (modify)
- cmd/evaluation.go (modify)
- cmd/contributor.go (modify)
- cmd/workscope.go (modify)

## What to do

When a user presses Ctrl+C during an interactive `huh` form prompt, `huh.Run()` returns `huh.ErrUserAborted`. Currently many call sites do not check for this error and propagate it as a raw error message to the user.

Find every call to `.Run()` on a `huh.NewInput()`, `huh.NewText()`, `huh.NewSelect()`, `huh.NewConfirm()`, or `huh.NewForm()` in the `cmd/` package. For each one, add a check:

```go
if err := huh.NewInput().Title("...").Value(&val).Run(); err != nil {
    if errors.Is(err, huh.ErrUserAborted) {
        return fmt.Errorf("cancelled")
    }
    return err
}
```

### How to find all locations:

```bash
grep -n "\.Run()" cmd/*.go | grep -v "_test.go" | grep -v "func "
```

Look for patterns like:
- `huh.NewInput().*.Run()`
- `huh.NewText().*.Run()`
- `huh.NewSelect().*.Run()`
- `huh.NewForm().*.Run()`

For each, check if the error return is handled. If the error is checked but doesnt test for `ErrUserAborted`, add the check.

### Import needed:

Add `"errors"` to the import block of each modified file (if not already present).
Add `"github.com/charmbracelet/huh"` to the import block (likely already present).

## Test

```bash
make test
```

Must exit 0.

## Dont
- Do NOT change any non-error-handling logic
- Do NOT change the form field definitions or validation
- Do NOT change function signatures
- Do NOT add new interactive prompts
notes: Blocked: Environment issues preventing completion. Tests hang indefinitely (go test timeout), builds timeout after 2+ minutes, and file edits are not persisting (git working tree shows clean after edits). Attempted with both default Go and Go 1.25rc1. All required code changes were identified and attempted across 9 files (activity.go, measurement.go, funding.go, rights.go, attachment.go, collection.go, evaluation.go, contributor.go, workscope.go) to add errors.Is(err, huh.ErrUserAborted) checks with fmt.Errorf('cancelled') returns for all .Run() calls on huh forms.
close_reason: ea2683c Handle huh.ErrUserAborted in all interactive form .Run() calls

title: Fix selectLocations to return partial results on cancel
close_reason: 19db673 Fix selectLocations to return partial results on cancel

title: Document plaintext password storage and add warning to login output
description:
## Files
- internal/atproto/auth.go (modify)
- AGENTS.md (modify)
- README.md (modify)

## What to do

The app password is stored in plaintext in `~/.local/state/hc/auth-session.json`. While the file has 0600 permissions, this should be documented prominently and a warning shown at login time.

### 1. Add warning comment in auth.go

Add a prominent comment above the AuthSession struct:

```go
// AuthSession stores the authentication state.
// WARNING: The app password is stored in plaintext at ~/.local/state/hc/auth-session.json
// with 0600 permissions. This is required for automatic token refresh.
// Users should use ATProto app passwords (not their main password).
// TODO: Consider OS keychain integration for password storage.
type AuthSession struct {
```

### 2. Print warning after successful login

In the `PersistAuthSession` function or in the login command handler, after saving the session, print:

```go
fmt.Fprintf(w, "⚠ Session saved to %s (includes app password in plaintext)\n", sessionPath)
fmt.Fprintf(w, "  Tip: Use an ATProto app password, not your main account password\n")
```

Find where the login success message is printed (likely in `cmd/account.go` `runAccountLogin`) and add this warning there.

### 3. Update AGENTS.md

Fix the session path reference (line ~229):
- Change `~/.local/state/hyper/auth-session.json` to `~/.local/state/hc/auth-session.json`
- Add a note about plaintext password storage

### 4. Update README.md

In the authentication section, add a security note:

```markdown
> **Security Note:** Your app password is stored in plaintext at `~/.local/state/hc/auth-session.json`.
> Always use an [ATProto app password](https://bsky.app/settings/app-passwords), never your main account password.
```

## Test

```bash
make test
```

Must exit 0.

## Dont
- Do NOT change the actual storage mechanism (that would be a larger refactor)
- Do NOT remove the password from the session file (it is needed for token refresh)
- Do NOT change the file permissions (0600 is correct)
close_reason: 31a5570 Document plaintext password storage and add warning to login output
